value.title: AI Race Sends Big Tech’s Capital Spending to Stratospheric High
value.content: Four of the biggest US technology companies together have forecast capital expenditures that will reach about $650 billion in 2026 — a mind-boggling tide of cash earmarked for new data centers and the long list of equipment needed to make them tick, including artificial intelligence chips, networking cables and backup generators.

value.title: Wall Street’s Favorite Trades Collapse as Market Selloff Deepens
value.content: All across Wall Street, day by day, the headlong rush into the most popular trades, from tech stocks to gold to cryptocurrencies, has given way to a sudden retreat from risk.

value.title: Michigan AG Urges Revisit on Oracle Data Center Power Plan
value.content: Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel moved for a reconsideration of the approval for utility DTE Energy to power a massive data center development planned by Oracle and OpenAI and called the companies “untrustworthy” in a statement Thursday.

value.title: SoftBank Taps on OpenAI Investment, While Japanese Automakers Drag
value.content: SoftBank Group Corp.’s third-quarter earnings next week will be the first opportunity for the Japanese tech giant to respond to reports it’s considering raising its investment in OpenAI.

value.title: Coupang’s Influence Campaign Creates Powerful Washington Allies
value.content: A closed-door congressional meeting this week with South Korea’s top trade official was dominated by one topic: Coupang Inc., the Seattle-headquartered e-commerce platform best known as the “Amazon of South Korea.”

value.title: Uber Jury Awards $8.5 Million Damages in Sexual Assault Case
value.content: Uber Technologies Inc. was hit with $8.5 million in damages in a trial over a 19-year-old woman’s claim that she was raped by her driver in Arizona, raising the rideshare giant’s risk of a costly settlement to resolve thousands of similar cases.

value.title: Xero CEO: Our Company Can't Be Easily Cloned With AI
value.content: Sukhinder Singh Cassidy, CEO of Xero, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss how the firm's small business tools won't be overtaken, but rather enhanced by AI. Cassidy mentions that Xero sees AI as a net benefit for its product suite because the firm builds its LLM "on the back of" AI models. Her comments come as AI fears consume investors in software stocks. Cassidy speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

value.title: BetMGM CEO: Super Bowl is Our ‘Peak Moment'
value.content: Adam Greenblatt, CEO of BetMGM, discusses the company’s record 2025 performance and expectations for Super Bowl weekend. He tells Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close” that there’s concerns over the sports Prediction market. (Source: Bloomberg)

value.title: Amazon Falls After Vow to Spend $200 Billion on AI This Year
value.content: Amazon.com Inc. shares dropped after the company announced plans to spend $200 billion this year on data centers, chips and other equipment, worrying investors that its colossal bet on artificial intelligence may not pay off in the long run.

value.title: Atlassian CEO: AI One of The Best Things That Ever Happened to Us
value.content: Mike Cannon-Brookes, Atlassian CEO, discusses the company’s strong financial performance and revenue growth. He tells Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close” that AI is one of the best things that has ever happened to Atlassian. (Source: Bloomberg)

value.title: BYD’s $60 Billion Wipeout Points to Deeper Turmoil for China EVs
value.content: A relentless selloff in BYD Co. shares is laying bare investor anxiety over the profit outlook for China’s electric-vehicle sector, as cooling demand at home and surging raw material costs trigger a brutal reset of expectations.

value.title: XPO CEO: Early Innings of Industrial Demand Recovery
value.content: Mario Harik, Chairman and CEO of XPO, says he’s confident in the company’s outlook after a strong earnings beat and record-high share price. He speaks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

value.title: Tapestry CEO: We Are Targeting Younger Customers
value.content: Joanne Crevoiserat, CEO of Tapestry, discusses the company’s strong second-quarter results, citing record sales and earnings. She tells Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close” that Tapestry remains focused on disciplined brand building and reaching younger consumers. (Source: Bloomberg)

value.title: Blackstone-Backed Liftoff Postpones IPO on Software Rout
value.content: Blackstone Inc.-backed Liftoff Mobile Inc. postponed its initial public offering, after tech stocks spiraled on concerns over the impact of artificial intelligence on operating companies.

value.title: Bob's Discount Furniture CEO on $331M IPO
value.content: Bill Barton, President and CEO of Bob’s Discount Furniture, says the company’s IPO proceeds will be used to pay off all long-term debt, allowing it to operate as a debt-free public company. He speaks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

value.title: Apple Is Scaling Back Plans for New AI-Based Health Coach Service
value.content: Apple Inc. is scaling back plans for a virtual health coach, according to people with knowledge of the matter, part of an effort to rethink how the company approaches the burgeoning market for wellness services.

value.title: Oracle Blockbuster Bond Sale to Spur AI Borrowing Rush, Goldman Says
value.content: The relief in debt markets after Oracle’s record-setting bond deal has primed the pump for other tech giants looking to raise hundreds of billions of dollars for data-center infrastructure, according to the syndicate desk at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

value.title: Tether Co-Founder Weighs In on Bitcoin Selloff
value.content: Tether co-founder William Quigley weighs in on the recent selloff in cryptocurrencies. Speaking on "Bloomberg The Close," Quigley says he never expected crypto to be a long-term investment opportunity. (Source: Bloomberg)

value.title: Ives Says He's Never Seen a Software Selloff Like This
value.content: Dan Ives of Wedbush Securities says he's never seen a structural software stock selloff like this in 25 years, but he's still bullish on tech stocks. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

value.title: Gambling Pros Adjust to a Super Bowl on the Prediction Markets
value.content: The sudden rise of sites like Kalshi and Polymarket has sophisticated bettors scrambling to adjust their strategies.
